event: Facial nerve paresis in medical history
Gave birth to healthy twins at 35 weeks gestation
Loss of consciousness persisted for about 2 min
Intravenous infusion of magnesium sulphate (4 g)
Bilateral loss of vision at insecured light perception
Normal pupillary responses to a light stimulus
MSCT scan showed hypodensity of posterior white matter corresponding to vasogenic edema
MRI showed hyperintense signals in white matter predominantly in parietal and occipital regions
MRI findings characteristic of vasogenic edema typical for PRES
Blood pressure stabilized with enalapril maleate and methyldopa
Significant bilateral improvement of visual function up to 1.0 of best-corrected visual acuity
Normal findings from anterior eye segment and fundus
Visual field image showed bilaterally expressed peripheral relative scotoma
Visual field image showed depressed sensitivity of paracentral left visual field
Confirmed right-sided partial facial nerve paresis
Follow-up MRI showed significant regression of edema with persistence of discrete residual changes
Recommendation to take oral antihypertensive therapy (enalapril, 10 mg per day)
Recommendation to continue physical therapy for the paresis of the facial nerve
